summary: A shock February jobs print — the U.S. economy shed 92,000 jobs — collided with a Middle East-driven oil supply squeeze as the Strait of Hormuz closure sent energy prices surging. This toxic cocktail of slowing growth and rising prices has left the Fed paralyzed, effectively ending hopes for a rate cut at the FOMC meeting this week. The S&P 500 posted a 1.6% weekly loss and its first three-week losing streak in roughly a year.
tweet: A negative jobs print, a Hormuz oil shock, and a technical breakdown have shattered the soft-landing consensus — leaving every asset class on edge ahead of the Fed.
